description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, forming a robust and weathered structure. The archway is supported by two thick, rectangular piers, each resting on a broad base with subtle reddish accents at the corners. The arch itself is semi-circular, with a keystone at the apex that features a sculpted relief, possibly depicting a stylized face or mask. The stonework is irregular, giving the surface a rugged, ancient appearance, and the coloration ranges from dark gray to mossy green, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. There are no visible inscription panels or elaborate decorative motifs aside from the keystone relief. The overall form is simple yet monumental, evoking classical Roman architectural influences.
description: A weathered stone triumphal arch with thick piers, a semi-circular arch, and a sculpted keystone relief. The surface is rugged and aged, with dark gray and green tones.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full archway, thick piers, and the sculpted keystone relief at the top center.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth of the arch and the blocky construction of the piers.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the archway and piers but with less detail on the keystone relief.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the layered stone texture.
